meta.title: Healthy Meal Recipes
meta.disclaimer: Nutrition values are estimates based on standard ingredient quantities and typical brands. Actual values vary with portion size, substitutions, and exact products.

diet_tags.vegan: No animal products of any kind
diet_tags.vegetarian: No meat or fish; may include dairy and/or eggs
diet_tags.gluten_free: Gluten-free as written unless a swap is noted
diet_tags.dairy_free: Dairy-free as written unless a swap is noted

field_notes.gluten_free: Boolean reflects the recipe AS WRITTEN. See gluten_free_adaptable + notes for swaps.
field_notes.dairy_free: Boolean reflects the recipe AS WRITTEN. See dairy_free_adaptable + notes for swaps.

recipes.name: Lemon-Herb Baked Salmon with Quinoa & Asparagus
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F (205C). Cook quinoa in 1 cup water ~15 min, then fluff.
Place salmon and asparagus on a lined sheet pan. Drizzle with olive oil, garlic, lemon juice/zest, salt, and pepper.
Bake 12-15 min until salmon flakes easily and asparagus is tender-crisp.
Top salmon with fresh dill and serve over quinoa.
recipes.why_healthy: Omega-3 rich salmon, complete-protein quinoa, fiber from asparagus.

recipes.name: Chickpea & Quinoa Power Bowl with Tahini Dressing
ingredients: 1 can (15 oz) chickpeas, drained and rinsed
Dressing: 2 tbsp tahini, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 clove garlic, 2-3 tbsp water, salt
instructions: Cook quinoa in 1 cup water ~15 min; let cool slightly.
Optional: roast chickpeas at 425F for 20 min with olive oil and paprika for crunch.
Whisk tahini, lemon, garlic, water, and salt into a pourable dressing.
Assemble bowls with quinoa, spinach, tomatoes, cucumber, onion, and chickpeas. Drizzle with dressing.
recipes.why_healthy: Plant protein + fiber from chickpeas and quinoa, healthy fats from tahini.

recipes.name: Tofu & Broccoli Stir-Fry with Brown Rice
ingredients: 14 oz extra-firm tofu, cubed and pressed
3 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce or tamari
instructions: Cook brown rice per package (~40 min, or use a rice cooker).
Pan-sear tofu in a little oil until golden on all sides; set aside.
Stir-fry broccoli and pepper with ginger and garlic 4-5 min.
Add soy sauce, sesame oil, and cornstarch slurry; return tofu and toss until glossy. Serve over rice.
recipes.why_healthy: High plant protein, whole-grain rice, minimal added fat.
recipes.notes: Use tamari instead of soy sauce to make this gluten-free.

recipes.name: Red Lentil & Coconut Curry
instructions: Saute onion, garlic, and ginger until soft. Stir in curry powder and turmeric for 1 min.
Add lentils, tomatoes, coconut milk, and broth. Simmer 18-20 min until lentils break down.
Stir in spinach until wilted. Season with salt; serve over rice or with naan.
recipes.why_healthy: Lentils provide protein, iron, and fiber; turmeric and ginger are anti-inflammatory.

recipes.name: Greek Yogurt Breakfast Bowl with Berries & Almonds
instructions: Spoon yogurt into a bowl.
Top with berries, almonds, and chia seeds.
Drizzle with honey if desired and serve.
recipes.why_healthy: High-protein yogurt, antioxidant berries, no added sugar (honey optional).
recipes.notes: Swap in a plant-based yogurt to make this vegan/dairy-free (macros will shift).

recipes.name: Mediterranean Baked Cod with Tomatoes & Olives
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F (205C).
Place cod in a baking dish; surround with tomatoes, olives, and garlic.
Drizzle with olive oil, lemon, and oregano; season with salt and pepper.
Bake 18-20 min until cod is opaque and flakes. Garnish with parsley.
recipes.why_healthy: Lean white fish, low-carb, heart-healthy olive oil and vegetables.

recipes.name: Grilled Chicken & Vegetables with Roasted Sweet Potato
ingredients: 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts (6 oz each)
1 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp garlic powder
instructions: Preheat oven to 425F (220C). Toss sweet potatoes with 1 tbsp oil and roast 25 min.
Season chicken with paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper; grill or pan-sear ~6 min per side to 165F internal.
Saute zucchini and pepper in remaining oil until tender-crisp.
Slice chicken and plate with vegetables and sweet potato.
recipes.why_healthy: Lean protein, complex carbs from sweet potato, no refined ingredients.

recipes.name: Ahi Tuna Poke Bowl
ingredients: 1 cup dry sushi or brown rice
Marinade: 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp rice vinegar, scallions, sesame seeds
instructions: Cook rice and let cool slightly.
Toss tuna gently in the marinade; chill 10 min. Keep tuna cold and use it the same day.
Build bowls with rice, tuna, avocado, edamame, cucumber, and carrot.
Garnish with extra scallions and sesame seeds.
recipes.why_healthy: Lean raw tuna, healthy fats from avocado, balanced macros.
recipes.notes: Use tamari instead of soy sauce to make this gluten-free. Requires sushi-grade fish kept cold and used same-day.

recipes.name: Black Bean & Sweet Potato Tacos
ingredients: 1 can (15 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed
1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp chili powder, 1/2 tsp smoked paprika
Lime, cilantro, and salsa to serve
instructions: Preheat oven to 425F (220C). Toss sweet potato with oil and spices; roast 25 min.
Warm black beans with a pinch of cumin and salt.
Char tortillas in a dry pan.
Fill tortillas with sweet potato, beans, avocado, cilantro, salsa, and a squeeze of lime.
recipes.why_healthy: High fiber and plant protein, beta-carotene from sweet potato, no animal fat.

recipes.name: Turkey & Quinoa Stuffed Bell Peppers
ingredients: 4 large bell peppers, halved and seeded
instructions: Preheat oven to 375F (190C). Cook quinoa in 1.5 cups water ~15 min.
Brown turkey with onion and garlic; add tomatoes, cumin, oregano, and cooked quinoa.
Stuff pepper halves with the mixture; top with cheese if using.
Bake 25-30 min until peppers are tender.
recipes.why_healthy: Lean ground turkey, fiber-rich quinoa and peppers, portion-controlled.
recipes.notes: Omit the cheese to make this dairy-free.

recipes.name: Shrimp & Zucchini Noodle Bowl with Pesto
ingredients: 12 oz raw shrimp, peeled and deveined
instructions: Saute shrimp with garlic in olive oil 2-3 min per side until pink; remove.
Add zucchini noodles to the pan and cook 2 min until just tender (do not overcook).
Toss noodles with pesto and tomatoes; return shrimp.
Finish with lemon and black pepper.
recipes.why_healthy: High protein, very low carb, vegetable-based noodles.
recipes.notes: Use a dairy-free / vegan pesto to make this dairy-free.

recipes.name: Spinach, Mushroom & Feta Egg-White Frittata
instructions: Preheat oven to 375F (190C). In an oven-safe skillet, saute onion and mushrooms until soft.
Add spinach until wilted.
Whisk eggs and egg whites with salt and pepper; pour over vegetables. Sprinkle feta on top.
Cook 2 min on the stovetop, then transfer to the oven 15 min until set. Slice into 4 wedges.
recipes.why_healthy: High protein, low carb, loaded with vegetables; great make-ahead meal.
recipes.notes: Omit feta for dairy-free (macros will shift).
